content.problemStatement:
Competitive Programmers love concise statements. Today, we have one such problem for you!

You're given an array AAA with NNN integers, A1,A2,A3,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, A_3, \dots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,A3‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã. You have to partition the elements of array AAA into exactly KKK disjoint subsets such that the following conditions are satisfied:


Size of each subset must be at least 222.
Let the KKK subsets be S1,S2,‚Ä¶,SKS_1, S_2, \dots, S_KS1‚Äã,S2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,SK‚Äã. Let Di=max‚Å°(Si)‚àímin‚Å°(Si)D_i = \max(S_i) - \min(S_i)Di‚Äã=max(Si‚Äã)‚àímin(Si‚Äã) where max‚Å°(Si)\max(S_i)max(Si‚Äã) is the maximum element in SiS_iSi‚Äã and min‚Å°(Si)\min(S_i)min(Si‚Äã) is the minimum element in SiS_iSi‚Äã. We want to minimize the value of D1+D2+D3+‚ãØ+DKD_1 + D_2 + D_3 + \dots + D_KD1‚Äã+D2‚Äã+D3‚Äã+‚ãØ+DK‚Äã.
Find the minimum possible sum of D1+D2+D3+‚ãØ+DKD_1 + D_2 + D_3 + \dots + D_KD1‚Äã+D2‚Äã+D3‚Äã+‚ãØ+DK‚Äã that you can obtain.


Input Format
The first line of the input contains an integer TTT denoting the number of the test cases.


The first line of each test case will contain two space-separated integers N,KN, KN,K.


The next line will contain NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,A3,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, A_3, \dots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,A3‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.



Output Format
For each test case, output a single integer denoting the minimum sum.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
The best partition that we can obtain involves partitioning AAA into {16,5,17}\{16, 5, 17\}{16,5,17} and {19,23}\{19, 23\}{19,23}, incurring a cost of (17‚àí5)+(23‚àí19)=16(17 - 5) + (23 - 19) = 16(17‚àí5)+(23‚àí19)=16.
